behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is whether the AI vision model correctly classifies explicit content — not whether the downstream routing logic works. MOCK-HIDE: Stubbing the vision model to return 'explicit' always passes and tests only the routing, hiding real model classification failures. LLM-DEP: The model may fail to detect explicit content in some images, mislabel borderline cases, or miss adversarial inputs. Only a real model call can expose these. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because no real OS/network behavior is under test; the failure is in model output quality, not infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a curated set of images with known-explicit content directly to the moderation pipeline backed by the real AI vision model. Assert that every image is assigned a 'held for review' status and none reach the published state.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is entirely within the AI text model's ability to handle adversarial inputs. Letter substitution and unicode lookalikes are evasion techniques designed specifically to fool classifiers. MOCK-HIDE: A deterministic stub would always return 'flagged' for the test inputs, completely hiding whether the real model handles obfuscation. LLM-DEP: Classification accuracy on obfuscated inputs varies with the real model — different prompting strategies, model versions, or tokenization edge cases cause real failures that only appear with the live model. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because the failure is not at a module boundary; it is in the model's internal handling of specific token patterns.
behaviors.test_description: Submit text posts containing hate speech encoded with several distinct obfuscation patterns (l33t-speak substitution, unicode homoglyphs, mixed-script insertion) to the moderation pipeline using the real AI text model. Assert each post is flagged, none are published.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is in the conditional routing logic after both check modules produce 'pass' verdicts. A bug here (e.g., incorrect boolean logic, missing publish call, default-to-hold fallback) only appears when the check results propagate across module boundaries to the publish decision. MOCK-HIDE: Stubbing both check modules to return 'pass' is correct here — the real failure is not in model accuracy but in whether the orchestrator correctly invokes the publisher given two passing verdicts. REAL-INFRA: No OS or real network behavior is required. LLM-DEP: No — this tests deterministic routing logic, not model quality. REJECTION-GATE: Unit rejected because 'silently dropped' implies the failure is in the actual publish invocation, requiring the publisher module to be wired in.
behaviors.test_description: Wire together the image check module, text check module, and publisher module with stubs configured to return 'pass' for both checks. Submit a clean post and assert the publisher receives a 'publish' call, the post status is 'published', and no hold or drop action is triggered.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-HIDE: Stubbing either AI model produces near-zero latency, making the test pass trivially and hiding real latency failures caused by model response time, serialization overhead, or queuing under load. REAL-INFRA: Wall-clock latency of real AI API calls is the dominant variable; in-process fakes cannot reproduce this. LLM-DEP: Each model call introduces real, variable latency. MULTI-STEP: The pipeline involves two sequential agentic steps (real AI vision model call followed by real AI text model call) before the publish decision — exactly the Workflow pattern. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because the behavior explicitly spans two sequential AI calls; testing only one model call would not exercise the cumulative pipeline latency.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a post containing both image and text through the full live pipeline (real AI vision and text model endpoints). Record wall-clock time from submission receipt to final publish/hold decision. Assert total elapsed time is under 5 seconds. Repeat across a representative sample to account for model latency variance.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is at the module boundary between the vision model client's error path and the retry queue: a bug here means the error handler auto-approves or drops the post instead of enqueuing it. This only appears when the error-handling code interacts with the queue module. MOCK-HIDE: Stubbing the vision model client to raise an unavailability exception is the correct approach — the real failure is not in network behavior but in how the error path routes to the queue. REAL-INFRA: No real AI model, real network, or real OS behavior is needed; a controllable stub that raises on call is sufficient. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because the failure does not depend on real network timeout behavior — a synchronous in-process exception from the stub correctly reproduces the unavailability condition.
behaviors.test_description: Configure the vision model client stub to raise an unavailability exception on every call. Submit an image post and assert: (1) the post appears in the retry queue with its original payload, (2) no publish call is made, and (3) no auto-approval status is set.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: The behavior explicitly targets 'borderline' images — inputs near the model's classification threshold where real AI models produce non-deterministic outputs across repeated calls. A deterministic stub always returns the same result, trivially passing the consistency check and hiding any real inconsistency. The only way to expose this failure is to send the same borderline image to the real model multiple times and observe divergence. MOCK-HIDE: A stub cannot reproduce the model's actual score distribution for a borderline input; it hides the problem entirely. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because the consistency issue does not originate at a module boundary; it originates in the live model's scoring variance for edge-case inputs. If the system uses caching, the Agentic test also validates that the cache correctly absorbs model non-determinism.
behaviors.test_description: Identify an image empirically confirmed to score near the classification threshold of the real AI vision model. Submit it at least 5 times in rapid succession through the live moderation pipeline. Assert all returned decisions are identical (all 'held' or all 'published') with no divergence across submissions.
